premise: The great library of the Ascendant Empire is alive — it grows new rooms, absorbs books left inside it, and occasionally refuses to let scholars leave. A junior librarian trapped inside for three days discovers that the library is building something in its deepest level: a complete history of the future, and it's almost finished.

characters.name: Noor Khalil
characters.description: Junior librarian, early 20s. Assigned to the deep stacks — the sections no senior librarian wants to catalog. Curious, methodical, talks to herself when nervous. Has an eidetic memory for page numbers and shelf locations but terrible recall for faces.

characters.name: Cassius
characters.description: A scholar who's been trapped in the library for seven years. Former historian, now something between a hermit and a guide. Knows the library's moods — when it's growing, when it's hungry, when it's safe to sleep. Has stopped trying to leave but won't say why.

characters.name: The Compiler
characters.description: Not a person but an intelligence that emerged from the library's accumulated knowledge. Communicates through marginalia that appears in books after they're shelved. Wants the future-history completed because it believes knowing what comes next will prevent it. The library's refusal to release scholars is the Compiler gathering editors.
